rulings.Case Title: MOHSIN AHMMED VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mohsin Ahmmed'S Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2015, Line 1, Plaintiff Mohsin Ahmmed'S Motion To Compel Further Responses Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN JOHNSON, ON BEHALF OF THEMSELVES AND ALL et al VS. MOBILITY PLUS TRANSPORTATION LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 8, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KEVIN JOHNSON, ON BEHALF OF THEMSELVES AND ALL, CARL STOREY, JR ON BEHALF OF THEMSELVES AND ALL Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement. Denied without prejudice. The settlement amounts are based on a formula based on work days. Eligible workdays for the San Francisco Terminal Class is 50,000, and 21,200 for the Martinez Terminal Class. No information is provided with respect to what actual workdays were put in by either class. There is insufficient information about the distribution of hours worked among the workers. Workers who put in only a few hours would have little incentive to join the class. The Court finds this significant because the amount of the settlement will reduce to 50% based on the number of claims submitted. The Court questions the basis for attorneys fees of one-third rather than 25% of the settlement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL et al VS. IRELAND INTERIOR SYSTEMS, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Index
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 8, 2015, Line 12, PLAINTIFF MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL, MICHAEL VAN BEMMEL, GLENDA VAN BEMMEL, RICHARD WOODS, ANNETTE WAGNER, CARLOS INTERIOR SYSTEMS, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORAT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adjudication. Denied. Plaintiff met her initial burden as to all three causes of action. Defendant has raised triable issues of material fact as to all three causes of action. As to the breach of contract cause of action, the Declaration of Michael Ireland raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Michelle and Michael Van Bemmel had authority to contract on behalf of Ireland Interior Systems. (M. Ireland Decl., pars. 4, 6, 11.) The existence of agency, as well as the extent of authority of an agent, are questions of fact. (Wickham v. Southland Corp. (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 49, 55.) As to the unjust enrichment/restitution cause of action, the Declaration of Michael Ireland raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Ireland Interior Systems knowingly accepted the benefit. (M. Ireland Decl., par. 11; see Hernandez v. Lopez (2009) 180 Cal.App.4th 932, 938-39.) The Declaration of Jeri Ireland also raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Ireland Interior Systems benefitted from the funds. (J. Ireland Decl., pars. 4-5, 6-8.) As to the common counts cause of action, the Declaration of Michael Ireland raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was a valid statement of indebtedness between Plaintiff and Ireland Interior Systems. (M. Ireland Decl., pars. 4, 6, 11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES LLC VS. KIMBERLY SILVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order That Matters In Req. For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Deft Kimberly Silva
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES LLC Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order That Matters In Req. For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Deft Kimberly Silva Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.
